---
title: "Exercise 3 — Local Git Workflow"
format: html
---

**Module 2 | ~35 minutes**

---

## Goal

Practice the core local Git workflow: `init` → `add` → `commit` → `log` → `diff`.

---

## Part A — Create a local repository

```bash
cd ~/Downloads

mkdir YOUR_USERNAME
cd YOUR_USERNAME

git init
ls -a     # confirm .git was created
```

---

## Part B — First commit

```bash
nano README.md
```

Write a brief introduction about yourself. Save and close: `Ctrl+X → Y → Enter`

```bash
git status           # git sees the file but isn't tracking it
git add README.md
git status           # now it's staged
git commit -m "added README with introduction"
git log
```

---

## Part C — More commits

```bash
nano education.md    # list your education history
git add education.md
git commit -m "added education history"

nano README.md       # paste the education content at the bottom
git add README.md
git commit -m "merged education into README"

rm education.md
git add education.md
git commit -m "removed separate education file"

git log --oneline    # see all 4 commits
```

---

## Part D — Inspect changes with diff

```bash
nano README.md       # add your hobbies at the bottom
```

Before staging:

```bash
git diff             # working directory vs staging area
```

After staging:

```bash
git add README.md
git diff --staged    # staging area vs last commit
git commit -m "added hobbies section"
```

Compare first and last commit:

```bash
git log --oneline           # note the two hashes
git diff FIRST_HASH LAST_HASH
```

---

## What you learned

| Command | What it does |
|---------|-------------|
| `git init` | Create a repository |
| `git status` | See what has changed |
| `git add <file>` | Stage a file |
| `git commit -m "msg"` | Save a snapshot |
| `git log --oneline` | View history |
| `git diff` | See unstaged changes |
| `git diff --staged` | See staged changes |
